title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Buying Term Insurance? Don't Ignore Claims Settlement Ratio
When planning to buy life cover, many people first compare premium rates. However, something even more important is the term insurance claim settlement ratio. It shows how often an insurer pays claims after the policyholder’s death. A higher term insurance claim settlement ratio may mean a better chance for your family to receive the payout smoothly.
It is natural to want an affordable term plan, but it is just as important to check the company's term plan claim settlement ratio before making a decision. Choosing a plan based only on the premium may not offer your family full protection. Understanding how the claim settlement ratio works, where to find it, and why it matters can help you select the right term insurance.
This blog explains the importance of claim settlement ratios in simple terms, so you can make an informed choice for your family's financial safety.
What Is a Claim Settlement Ratio?
The term insurance claim settlement ratio shows how many claims an insurance company has paid compared to how many claims it received in one year. It is expressed as a percentage. For example, if a company receives 100 claims and settles 98, its claim settlement ratio will be 98%.
A high term plan claim settlement ratio usually means the insurer is more reliable. It shows that the company tries its best to honor claims and support families in their time of need. However, it is important not to depend only on the CSR when choosing an insurer. Good customer service, simple processes, and the honesty of the company are also important.
Checking the term insurance claim settlement ratio helps you feel confident that your family may not face problems when making a claim. It is one of the many important points to think about while buying term insurance.
How to Calculate Term Insurance Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR)
Calculating the term insurance claim settlement ratio is very simple. It is done using a basic formula. You divide the number of claims that the company settled by the number of claims it received and then multiply the result by 100.
Here’s the formula:CSR = (Total Claims Settled ÷ Total Claims Received) × 100
For example:
If a company received 500 claims in a year
And settled 475 claims
The calculation would be: (475 ÷ 500) × 100 = 95%
This means the company paid 95% of the claims it received.
You can check the CSR of different companies every year in the Annual Report published by the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI). It is better to look at the 5-year average to get a more accurate idea of how well the company handles claims.
How to Find the CSR of an Insurance Company?
Finding the term plan claim settlement ratio of an insurance company is very easy. Every year, the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) publishes an official report. This report lists the claim settlement ratios of all registered insurance companies in India.
You can simply visit the IRDAI website and check their latest Annual Report. It will have all the updated information.
While checking the report, it is better to look at the 5-year average CSR, not just the most recent year. This gives a clearer picture of the company's overall performance. A company may have a good CSR one year, but not every year. Checking the 5-year average helps you find a stable and trustworthy company.
This way, you can choose an insurance company that is more likely to settle claims easily and support your family when needed.
Can a High CSR Always Ensure Claim Settlement?
A high term insurance claim settlement ratio is a good sign, but it does not always guarantee that your claim will be paid.
There are a few important reasons why claims can still get rejected:
Incorrect medical information: If you hide health problems like diabetes or heart disease, the claim may be denied later.
Lifestyle habits not shared: If you do not tell about smoking or drinking habits, it may cause problems.
Incorrect or incomplete documents: Missing important papers or giving wrong details can delay or even reject the claim.
Policy lapse: If you miss paying your premiums on time, your policy can become invalid, and the claim may be rejected.
To avoid any issues, always fill in your forms honestly, share complete information, and check your documents carefully before submitting them. This way, you can help your family have a smooth claim experience later.
What Is the Importance of Term Insurance Claim Settlement Ratio?
The term insurance claim settlement ratio is very important when you are choosing a life insurance plan. It is not just a number. It tells you how reliable an insurance company might be in giving money to your family if something happens to you.
Here’s why it matters:
1. Helps Secure Family's Financial FutureThe biggest reason for buying a term plan is to make sure your loved ones are financially safe even if you are not around. A company with a high term insurance claim settlement ratio is more likely to pay the sum assured quickly. Your family may not have to struggle or wait for the claim amount.
2. Shows Trustworthiness of InsurerA good term plan claim settlement ratio shows that the company is trustworthy. It means they have honored most of the claims they received. You would want a company that keeps its promises and supports families during hard times.
3. Affects Mental PeaceBeing insured by a company that settles claims well provides you with certainty and peace of mind. With such insurance, you can rest assured knowing your family will receive support from your chosen company at their time of need.
4. Guides Your Buying DecisionTwo insurance providers sometimes provide the same premium payment amount to customers. The term insurance claim settlement ratio should be checked to help users make their decision. You would get better security from an insurer who has a better ratio during claim settlement.
5. Reduces Risk of Claim RejectionEven though CSR alone does not promise that every claim will be settled, companies with consistently high CSR usually have better customer service, easier claim processes, and fewer problems. This can lower the chance of your claim being rejected later.
Thus, when you buy a term insurance plan, do not look at only the price. Always check the company's claim settlement record to make a safer and smarter choice for your family.
What Is the Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR) by Number?
The term insurance claim settlement ratio by number shows the percentage of claims an insurance company has settled compared to the number of claims it received during a year. It simply tells you how often the insurer honors claims.
The formula is:CSR by Number = (Total Claims Settled ÷ Total Claims Received) × 100
For example, if a company receives 1,000 claims and pays 950, the CSR by number will be: (950 ÷ 1,000) × 100 = 95%.
This figure is useful because it shows whether the insurer handles most of the claims fairly. However, it only tells you about the number of claims settled. It does not show the amount of money paid in each claim.
When comparing companies, checking the term insurance claim settlement ratio by number can give you a good idea of how reliable the company has been in settling claims.
What Is the Claim Settlement Ratio by Benefit Amount?
The term insurance claim settlement ratio by benefit amount shows how much of the total claim money an insurer has actually paid.
The formula is:CSR by Amount = (Total Amount Paid ÷ Total Amount Claimed) × 100
For example:
Total claims filed = ₹50 crore
Total claims paid = ₹47 crore
So, CSR by amount = (47 ÷ 50) × 100 = 94%
This ratio is important if you are choosing a high-value term plan. It tells you whether the insurer also settles big claims properly, not just small ones.
A company may have a good CSR by number but might pay less money compared to the claims raised. That’s why checking both types of claim settlement ratios give you a complete understanding of the insurer’s reliability before buying a term plan.
What Are the Factors That Influence Term Insurance Claim Settlement Ratio?
Many things affect the term insurance claim settlement ratio of an insurance company. Some are related to the policyholder, while others depend on the company’s own working style. Understanding these factors helps you know how claims are handled and why some claims get accepted quickly while others may get delayed or rejected.
1. Quality of Policyholders' DisclosuresWhen people buy a term insurance plan, they are asked about their health, habits, job, and other details. If someone shares full and true information, it becomes easier for the insurer to assess the risk and settle the claim later. Hiding any health problems or lifestyle habits like smoking can cause issues later, leading to claim rejections. Good disclosure from the start improves the company’s term plan claim settlement ratio.
2. Documentation ProcessClaim approval depends heavily on proper documentation methods. A timely submission of proper death certificates along with ID proofs and medical reports together with properly filled forms leads to a simplified claim process. The insurer faces delayed settlement of claims or claim rejection due to mistakes and wrong details and missing documents which negatively impacts their record of claim settlements.
3. Health DeclarationsThe process of claim settlement depends heavily on a person's medical record. A policyholder should disclose any present medical illnesses, together with surgical history at the time of purchasing the policy. The companies which receive honest health information from their customers trust them for transparency. Fast claim settlement by insurers depends on proper health declarations which leads to an elevated term insurance claim settlement ratio.
4. Timely Premium PaymentA term insurance policy needs regular premium payments to stay active. If a person misses the premium payment and the policy lapses, then no claim can be made. To ensure that claims are settled easily, it is important to pay all premiums on time and keep the policy active throughout its tenure.
5. Claim InvestigationSometimes, insurers investigate claims, especially if the policyholder passes away within the first two or three years of buying the plan. This is called an early death claim. In such cases, insurers check all facts carefully. If everything is found correct, the claim is paid. However, if something seems wrong, it could lead to delays or even rejections.
6. Size of the ClaimLarge claims trigger additional, stringent examination as a standard process by insurers. The settlement of steep payment amounts represents a logical process because such large financial obligations extend across significant monetary amounts. Once all provided documentation matches expectations and there are no incorrect disclosures, high-value insurance claims can be processed efficiently.
7. Company’s Internal ProcessesA company with strong systems, trained claims teams, and modern technology can settle claims faster and with fewer mistakes. Automated systems also help track and approve claims quickly. Companies that invest in good service tend to have a higher term insurance claim settlement ratio.
8. Regulatory EnvironmentUnder the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI), a strict rule system supports customer protection. Insurance organizations must adhere properly to these rules. The set guidelines guarantee insurance providers deliver fair treatment to their customers while ensuring prompt payment of valid claims. The implementation of robust regulation enhances CSR performance throughout the entire insurance sector.
In short, being honest while buying a policy, submitting proper documents, keeping the policy active, and choosing a company with strong processes can all help in improving claim settlement success. This makes sure that your family’s financial protection stays strong when it matters most.
Conclusion
The term insurance claim settlement ratio can be an important guide when buying life insurance. While premium affordability matters, CSR shows the trustworthiness of an insurance company.
Before you decide, always check the term plan claim settlement ratio for the last five years, not just the latest year. Make sure to be honest in your policy forms and maintain complete and updated documents.
Choosing a company with a high and steady CSR can help ensure that your loved ones get financial support easily and without stress. After all, a term plan is not just about low premiums – it is mainly about protecting your family’s future.
FAQs
How is the claim settlement ratio calculated?
The term insurance claim settlement ratio is calculated by dividing the number of claims settled by the total claims received and multiplying by 100.
What is the time frame within which the claim has to be reported?
There is no fixed time limit for reporting death claims. However, it is better to report as soon as possible. For disability claims, you must report within 120 days. For critical illness claims, within 60 days of diagnosis.
What are the documents required for claim settlement?
The documents needed usually include:
Death certificate
Policy document
ID proof of nominee
Medical records, if applicable
Claim form
Always check with the insurer for a full list.
Under what circumstances does the company decline the claim?
A claim may get declined if:
Wrong information was given while buying the policy (non-disclosure).
Important health history was hidden.
Premiums were unpaid, and the policy lapsed.
Who is entitled to receive the claim benefit?
The claim amount is paid to:
The nominee mentioned in the policy
A legal heir if no nominee was registered
The assignee, if the policy was assigned
The policyholder, in case of living benefit claims, like disability or critical illness
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. 4. Timely Premium Payment A term insurance policy needs regular premium payments to stay active. If a person misses the premium payment and the policy lapses, then no claim can be made. To ensure that claims are settled easily, it is important to pay all premiums on time and keep the policy active throughout its tenure.
5. Claim Investigation Sometimes, insurers investigate claims, especially if the life assured passes away within the first two or three years of buying the plan. This is called an early death claim. In such cases, insurers check all facts carefully. If everything is found correct, the claim is paid. However, if something seems wrong, it could lead to delays or even rejections.
6. Size of the Claim Large claims trigger additional, stringent examination as a standard process by insurers. The settlement of steep payment amounts represents a logical process because such large financial obligations extend across significant monetary amounts. 7. Company’s Internal Processes A company with strong systems, trained claims teams, and modern technology can settle claims faster and with fewer mistakes. Automated systems also help track and approve claims quickly. Companies that invest in good service tend to have a higher claim settlement ratio.
8. Regulatory Environment Under the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI), a strict rule system supports customer protection. Insurance organizations must adhere properly to these rules. The set guidelines guarantee insurance providers deliver fair treatment to their customers while ensuring prompt payment of valid claims. The implementation of robust regulation enhances CSR performance throughout the entire insurance sector.
In short, being honest while buying a policy, submitting proper documents, keeping the policy active, and choosing a company with strong processes can all help in improving claim settlement ratio . This makes sure that your family’s financial protection stays strong when it matters most.

The claim settlement ratio can be an important guide when buying life insurance. While premium affordability matters, CSR shows the trustworthiness of an insurance company.
Before you decide, always check the claim settlement ratio for the last five years, not just the latest year. Make sure to be honest in your policy forms and maintain complete and updated documents.
Choosing a company with a high and steady CSR can help ensure that your loved ones get financial support easily and without stress. After all, a term plan is not just about low premiums – it is mainly about protecting your family’s financial future.
FAQs
1. How is the claim settlement ratio calculated?
The claim settlement ratio is calculated by dividing the number of claims settled by the total claims received and multiplying by 100.
What are the documents required for claim settlement?
The documents needed usually include following, among others:
● Death certificate ● Policy document ● ID proof of nominee ● Medical records, if applicable ● Claim form
Always check with the insurer for a full list.

changes.pdf_final: ● Total claims filed = ₹50 crore ● Total claims paid = ₹47 crore ● So, CSR by amount = (47 ÷ 50) × 100 = 94%

changes.word_original: Total claims paid = ₹47 crore
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: So, CSR by amount = (47 ÷ 50) × 100 = 94%

changes.word_original: What Are the Factors That Influence Term Insurance Claim Settlement Ratio?
changes.pdf_final: What Are the Factors That Influence Claim Settlement Ratio?

changes.word_original: Many things affect the term insurance claim settlement ratio of an insurance company.
changes.pdf_final: Many things affect the claim settlement ratio of an insurance company.

changes.word_original: Quality of Policyholders' DisclosuresWhen people buy a term insurance plan, they are asked about their health, habits, job, and other details.
changes.pdf_final: Quality of Policyholders' Disclosures When people buy a term insurance plan, they are asked about their health, habits, job, and other details.

changes.word_original: Good disclosure from the start improves the company’s term plan claim settlement ratio.
changes.pdf_final: Good disclosure from the start improves the company’s claim settlement ratio.

changes.word_original: Documentation ProcessClaim approval depends heavily on proper documentation methods.
changes.pdf_final: Documentation Process Claim approval depends heavily on proper documentation methods.

changes.word_original: Health DeclarationsThe process of claim settlement depends heavily on a person's medical record.
changes.pdf_final: Health Declarations The process of claim settlement depends heavily on a person's medical record.

changes.word_original: Timely Premium PaymentA term insurance policy needs regular premium payments to stay active.
changes.pdf_final: Timely Premium Payment A term insurance policy needs regular premium payments to stay active.

changes.word_original: Claim InvestigationSometimes, insurers investigate claims, especially if the policyholder passes away within the first two or three years of buying the plan.
changes.pdf_final: Claim Investigation Sometimes, insurers investigate claims, especially if the life assured passes away within the first two or three years of buying the plan.

changes.word_original: Size of the ClaimLarge claims trigger additional, stringent examination as a standard process by insurers.
changes.pdf_final: Size of the Claim Large claims trigger additional, stringent examination as a standard process by insurers.

changes.word_original: Company’s Internal ProcessesA company with strong systems, trained claims teams, and modern technology can settle claims faster and with fewer mistakes.
changes.pdf_final: Company’s Internal Processes A company with strong systems, trained claims teams, and modern technology can settle claims faster and with fewer mistakes.

changes.word_original: Companies that invest in good service tend to have a higher term insurance claim settlement ratio.
changes.pdf_final: Companies that invest in good service tend to have a higher claim settlement ratio.

changes.word_original: Regulatory EnvironmentUnder the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI), a strict rule system supports customer protection.
changes.pdf_final: Regulatory Environment Under the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI), a strict rule system supports customer protection.

changes.word_original: In short, being honest while buying a policy, submitting proper documents, keeping the policy active, and choosing a company with strong processes can all help in improving claim settlement success.
changes.pdf_final: In short, being honest while buying a policy, submitting proper documents, keeping the policy active, and choosing a company with strong processes can all help in improving claim settlement ratio .

changes.word_original: The term insurance claim settlement ratio can be an important guide when buying life insurance.
changes.pdf_final: The claim settlement ratio can be an important guide when buying life insurance.

changes.word_original: Before you decide, always check the term plan claim settlement ratio for the last five years, not just the latest year.
changes.pdf_final: Before you decide, always check the claim settlement ratio for the last five years, not just the latest year.

changes.word_original: After all, a term plan is not just about low premiums – it is mainly about protecting your family’s future.
changes.pdf_final: After all, a term plan is not just about low premiums – it is mainly about protecting your family’s financial future.

changes.word_original: The term insurance claim settlement ratio is calculated by dividing the number of claims settled by the total claims received and multiplying by 100.
changes.pdf_final: The claim settlement ratio is calculated by dividing the number of claims settled by the total claims received and multiplying by 100.

changes.word_original: What is the time frame within which the claim has to be reported?
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: There is no fixed time limit for reporting death claims.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: However, it is better to report as soon as possible.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: For disability claims, you must report within 120 days.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: For critical illness claims, within 60 days of diagnosis.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF
